items.source: Avoma call 2026-05-04; no evidence of a subsequent call as of 2026-07-09

metrics.narrative: No quantified business case exists. Evan Bartholio described the pain qualitatively on the 2026-05-04 call ('we've been doing a lot of reporting on our own with data... tired of just looking at reports and looking at data') but no hours saved, cost, spend-under-management, or risk-exposure figures were ever discussed or requested. Metrics remain entirely undeveloped at this exploratory stage.

champion.narrative: Evan Bartholio is the strongest candidate: he leads AI initiatives within supply chain/operations, drove most of the questions on the 2026-05-04 call, and named specific modules he wants explored further. This is genuine curiosity, not yet proven advocacy: there is no evidence he has looped in colleagues, escalated internally, or re-engaged since 2026-05-04 despite committing to gather pain points from department colleagues before the next call. Status is developing, not confirmed.

competition.narrative: No named competing vendor has surfaced in any source. The live alternative is the status quo: Infor ERP as the system of record plus a separate system for supplier communications and a separate RFx/quoting tool, described directly by Evan Bartholio and Israel Powell on 2026-05-04. Given the deal has gone cooling since that call, 'do nothing' / stay with the current patchwork is the most credible current outcome absent renewed engagement.

identify_pain.narrative: Evan Bartholio articulated concrete, specific pain on 2026-05-04: supplier risk assessment today relies only on ERP/MRP historical data with no visibility into geopolitical or financial risk on direct suppliers ('we don't really have... a good pulse on other factors that might impact some supplier risk, whether that's, like, geopolitical stuff'); contract management is manual and requires looping in legal separately; and sourcing/quoting is handled through a patchwork of the Infor ERP plus a separate quoting/RFx tool. This is a real, owner-attributed pain statement, not an inferred one.

paper_process.narrative: No paper process discussion has occurred; the deal has not progressed past an exploratory overview call, far too early for contracting mechanics to surface.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer has been identified or engaged across either call (2026-05-01, 2026-05-04). The only two engaged contacts are Evan Bartholio (Business Systems Manager) and Israel Powell (Manager, Supply Chain Operations), both mid-level operational roles with no stated budget authority. Neither call surfaced a VP, CFO, or executive sponsor, and no subsequent contact has occurred to change this.

decision_process.narrative: No decision process has been described. Evan Bartholio characterized Rocket Lab as 'definitely at, like, an exploration phase, like, just seeing kind of what capabilities are out there' with no named committee, timeline, or approval chain. The only stated next step (a discovery call 'late this week or next week' from 2026-05-04) never materialized in the record.

decision_criteria.narrative: Evan Bartholio named three specific areas of interest on 2026-05-04: sourcing with autonomous negotiation (iSource/ANA), AI-driven supplier risk monitoring beyond ERP data (geopolitical and financial risk on direct suppliers), and Merlin Intake with the relevant stakeholder integration for guided buying. These are real, buyer-voiced evaluation priorities, but they have not been formalized into a documented requirements list or scored against alternatives.

critical_signals.text: With no vendor competitor in the picture, the real threat is inertia: Rocket Lab's current mix of Infor ERP plus manual and third-party tools remains the default outcome the longer this stays quiet.

The one substantive call on 4 May 2026 (about nine weeks ago) surfaced genuine interest in sourcing, supplier risk monitoring, and Merlin Intake, but no follow-up call has happened since despite a plan to book one within a week or two of that call.

A Govcloud/government-contract compliance question raised on 4 May 2026 still has no confirmed answer from Zycus, and it is a real potential blocker given Rocket Lab's government-contract constraints.
No new stakeholder has surfaced since the two contacts from the 4 May call; the buying group has not widened in over two months.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 2026-04-30, qualified same day, 69 days in Qualified stage with zero progression signals. Close date 2026-09-24 (78 days out) requires 10-11x the normal enterprise velocity from this stage. LastActivityDate 2026-05-05 (64 days ago); rep reached out 2026-06-22 with no buyer reply. Standard motion from Qualified to close is 12-15 months (discovery, demos, RFI/RFP, shortlist, workshops, commercials, negotiation, contracting); this deal has 78 days budgeted and has not yet held a discovery call. No submission-date milestones recorded. Forecast is Pipeline, which is appropriate, but the close date is fantasy.

Resolve the outstanding Govcloud/government-contract compliance question in writing; unanswered compliance concerns can silently stall public-sector-adjacent buyers like Rocket Lab.

competitors.name: Infor
competitors.quote: Yeah, I think so some of the systems we use currently, our MRP is in Infor, and we use some other, like, third parties as well as systems, like, to help manage, like, quoting.
competitors.how_we_win: Position the single integrated Zycus platform against the current fragmentation across ERP plus multiple point tools, emphasizing lower total cost of ownership versus maintaining separate systems for supplier communication, quoting, and risk tracking.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: An early-stage, single-threaded, exploratory deal that showed real promise in its one substantive call but has since gone quiet for over two months with rep commitments still outstanding and no economic buyer in sight.

deal_risk.text: Close date (24 Sep 2026, 77 days out) is not credible for a Qualified-stage deal that has not yet had a discovery call — the standard Zycus enterprise motion runs 12-15 months.

Govcloud/government-contract compliance question is unresolved — Evan Bartholio raised it on 2026-05-04 and Zycus has not delivered a confirmed answer; this could block progression given Rocket Lab's government-contract constraints.

win_position.text: No economic buyer engaged — only Evan Bartholio (Business Systems Manager) and Israel Powell (Manager, Supply Chain Operations) have appeared on any call (2026-05-01, 2026-05-04); no VP, CFO, or executive sponsor is on record.

Specific module interest named — Evan Bartholio said on 2026-05-04 that 'the sourcing, spy risk, and the Merlin intake... are probably the ones that resonate the most,' giving a concrete shortlist to build on.
Deal has stalled since the single discovery-style call — no follow-up has occurred in the roughly nine weeks since 2026-05-04 despite a stated plan to schedule one within a week or two.

Status quo is the only real alternative and the default outcome — Rocket Lab runs Infor ERP plus manual/third-party tools today, and with the deal cooling, 'do nothing' is the most likely path absent renewed engagement.

The deal has not yet crossed into confirmed-dark territory; it is cooling rather than abandoned, and a single re-engagement could restart it.

value.name: Status quo (Infor ERP + manual/third-party tools, do-nothing)
value.quote: Yeah, I think so some of the systems we use currently, our MRP is in Infor, and we use some other, like, third parties as well as systems, like, to help manage, like, quoting.
value.how_we_win: Position the single integrated Zycus platform against the current fragmentation across ERP plus multiple point tools, emphasizing lower total cost of ownership versus maintaining separate systems for supplier communication, quoting, and risk tracking.

value.name: Status quo (Infor ERP + manual/third-party tools, do-nothing)
value.quote: We've been doing a lot of reporting on our own with data, and we've gotten good at that. But it's like, alright, tired of just looking at reports and looking at data, let's have some automations.
value.how_we_win: Use the Merlin Intake / Ana agentic story to show a concrete before/after on the manual reporting and supplier-risk gap Evan already named, rather than competing against another vendor.

packets.subject: Status quo (Infor ERP + manual/third-party tools, do-nothing)
